hooks.prompt: Check: Does this file change hardcode any LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) instead of using the provider-agnostic router from src/lib/llm/executor.ts? Does it leak tenant data across boundaries? Does it use require() instead of import? If any violation, respond with BLOCK and explain why. Otherwise respond with ALLOW.
Check: Does this file import from AWS SDK, Vercel, Prisma, or any non-Cloudflare infrastructure? Global-Claw is Cloudflare-only. If violation found, respond BLOCK. Otherwise ALLOW.
If the command just executed was 'wrangler deploy', remind the user which environment was targeted (staging or production) and warn if it was production. Otherwise ALLOW.

Before compacting, ensure the summary preserves: (1) Which migration files have been created/applied, (2) Current state of wrangler.jsonc bindings, (3) Which API routes are implemented vs pending, (4) Any failing tests, (5) Current implementation phase from the execution plan.
